steps.title: Check current porter deployment
steps.description:
Verify your porter version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=porter
helm list -n porter 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working porter installation.

steps.title: Review porter configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant porter configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=porter
kubectl get configmap -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=porter
```
I was reading the PR for adding `porter parameters generate` (where we are considering adding branching logic to skip adding parameters or default them based on the values in the bundle) and realized that these interactive generation commands have

steps.title: Apply the fix for Rethink interactive commands?
steps.description:
# What does this change
 * Adds Parameter Set generation cmds/logic
 * Refactors previous generation logic into common code and credential-/parameter-specific logic

# What issue does it fix
Contributes towards https://github.com/deislabs/porter/issues/1053

# Notes for the reviewer
N/A
```yaml
porter credentials create NAME [--reference REFERENCE] [--dry-run] [--output yaml|plain]
porter parameters create NAME [--reference REFERENCE] [--dry-run] [--output yaml|plain]
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n porter -l app.kubernetes.io/name=porter
kubectl get events -n porter --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Rethink interactive commands?" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
# What does this change
 * Adds Parameter Set generation cmds/logic
 * Refactors previous generation logic into common code and credential-/parameter-specific logic

# What issue does it fix
Contributes towards https://github.com/deislabs/porter/issues/1053

# Notes for the reviewer
N/A
codeSnippets:
porter credentials create NAME [--reference REFERENCE] [--dry-run] [--output yaml|plain]
porter parameters create NAME [--reference REFERENCE] [--dry-run] [--output yaml|plain]
porter credentials create k8s -r example.com/kubernetes:v1.2.3 --dry-run -o json > k8s-creds.json

---
# Generated by Porter
# Credentials generated from example.com/kubernetes:v1.2.3
schemaVersion: 1.0.0-DRAFT+b6c701f
# Name of the credential set
name: k8s
custom:
  bundleReference: example.com/kubernetes:v1.2.3
created: '2021-06-30T17:11:34.075214-05:00'
modified: '2021-06-30T17:11:34.075214-05:00'
credentials:
# kubeconfig applies to: All Actions
- name: kubeconfig
  source:
    # Replace the value field below with any of the following: secret, env, path, command or value
    value: TODO
# token applies to: install, upgrade
- name: token
  source:
    # Replace the value field below with any of the following: secret, env, path, command or value
    value: TODO

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with porter installed or the issue environment reproducible.
